title: ophrys aranifera subsp. incubacea
intro: Ophrys aranifera subsp. incubacea, also known as the Spider Orchid or Incubus Spider Orchid, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the orchid family (Orchidaceae) that is native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ophrys aranifera subsp. incubacea include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its antiseptic and antibacterial properties, which can aid in wound healing and prevent infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used as a tea or tincture, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
